rationale: Codebase-pin re-verify at 0c6c5669. All three round-2 pins hold. (1) Refactor-first premise is code-exact: internal/instruct/panelstate.go PanelStateEntry.verdict() (:87-161) is a standalone, NON-delegating reproduction of the decision matrix — it inlines Abandoned->Warn, RoundMismatch->Block, staleness Block/Warn, !Complete->Block, Rejects/HardBlocks->Block, Approves>=thr->Pass, else Block, and never calls panel.PanelGateDecision. So R2's 'today it does NOT call PanelGateDecision, refactor it to delegate to ResolveGateFacts+PanelGateDecision and delete the copy' rests on a true premise; the delegation target (PanelGateDecision gate.go:131 returning Decision{Action}; ResolveGateFacts :349) is the confirmed single home. (2) Schema-doc AC symbols all present at this commit: panel.FileName const 'panel.json' (panel.go:32); panel.verdictFileRE var regexp ^(.+)-round-([1-9][0-9]*)\.json$ (tally.go:33, N>=1); panel.ConsolidatedName func -> consolidated-round-<N>.md (tally.go:38) — exactly what R4 / AC-99 pin, so TestPanelSchemaDoc_MatchesConstants has real anchors. (3) My round-1 placement nit is now folded into the Open Questions Placement note (line 120): it names the half-migrated tree, confirms ../../adr/ is written for the flat destination, and commits to reconciling the file to flat .mindspec/specs/ on the same rebase-forward before plan-approve — the exact reconciliation I asked for. No post-108/109 symbol asserted present. Ready.

findings.note: panelstate.go verdict() (:87-161) is a genuine second decision matrix, not a thin wrapper: it recomputes Warn/Block/Pass over the same facts (p.ApproveThreshold(), r.Complete(), r.RoundMismatch, r.Rejects/HardBlocks, staleness via e.LiveBranchSHA vs p.ReviewedHeadSHA) with zero reference to panel.PanelGateDecision. R2's 'collapse the duplicate onto the single home and delete verdict()' is therefore well-founded, and TestPanelStateVerdict_DelegatesToPanelGateDecision (AC line 98) is a meaningful falsifier — a surviving verdict() matrix breaks it.

verdictFileRE deliberately rejects -round-0 (regexp starts [1-9]) and consolidated-round-<N>.md (different extension) does not match it, per the tally.go:24-33 comment. AC-99's negative cases ('a -round-0 verdict file, a wrong extension, or a different consolidated prefix') map 1:1 to what the constants actually reject, so the doc-vs-constants parity test is real, not decorative. Loose phrasing 'code constants' for verdictFileRE(var)/ConsolidatedName(func) noted in round-1, unchanged and harmless for a doc authored from their shape.

The spec still physically sits at .mindspec/docs/specs/110-.../ at 0c6c5669 (not yet moved), so the ../../adr/ touchpoint links remain dead from the on-disk location today; but this is now an explicitly-scheduled rebase-forward action in the Placement note, non-blocking for spec-approve (no pre-110 touchpoint-link check exists, and R6-by-ID resolution via adr.Store flat-first still succeeds). The sequencing note discharges the round-1 ask; the physical move is a plan/rebase-time obligation, correctly deferred, not a spec defect.
